title: Mazalti Investimentos
description: I developed a modern and functional website for Mazalti Investimentos, a financial consultancy, using WordPress and Elementor. With a responsive design, SEO optimization, and RD Station integration, the site was created to attract and engage new investors, resulting in a significant increase in leads.
text: Mazalti Investimentos, a consultancy specializing in smart money, had the main goal of attracting new clients for its investment services. I was responsible for the complete development of the site, using WordPress and Elementor, with form integrations to RD Station for marketing campaign optimization.</br></br>Mazalti's target audience included young adults starting from 20 years old, interested in beginning their journey in the investment world. One of the biggest challenges in this project was creating the layout from a briefing, since I was used to working with ready-made layouts. However, by requesting references of sites the client liked, I managed to align expectations and deliver a design that not only pleased the client but was also effective in user acquisition.</br></br>The results were highly significant, with an increase in the number of leads after the site's launch, demonstrating the effectiveness of the design and the RD Station integration.

title: Miogx
description: I developed the website for Miogx, an online showcase for designer Miguel's work, focusing on Motion Design. Using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, jQuery, WordPress, and ACF, the site highlights the versatility of the client's projects and features optimized integration with Vimeo for displaying videos in various formats.
text: The Miogx project was developed aiming to create a digital showcase emphasizing the diverse works of Miguel, a designer specializing in Motion Design. The site was designed to function as a unified catalog, attracting companies interested in his services.</br></br>I used a combination of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and jQuery for the structure and interactivity, while WordPress, alongside Advanced Custom Fields (ACF), was chosen as the CMS to allow a more flexible management of the internal pages. A major challenge of this project was integrating videos hosted on Vimeo, considering the variety of formats and sizes. Adapting all these elements to ensure a fluid experience across different devices required close attention to detail and rigorous testing.</br></br>The site not only passed all Google metrics but was also very well received by the client, Miguel, who highlighted the ease of content management and the continuous support during development. His testimonial below reinforced satisfaction with the project.

title: Pokemon Angular
description: I developed a Pokedex using Angular and TypeScript to improve my skills with these technologies. The project lists pokemons by generation, featuring details like evolutions and shiny versions.
text: The 'Pokemon in Angular' project was developed to learn Angular with TypeScript and improve my design skills, without following a pre-defined layout. The Pokedex allows users to explore pokemons listed by generation, with detailed information about alternate versions, evolutions, shiny versions, and stats.</br></br>Built with HTML, CSS, Sass/SCSS, TypeScript, and Angular, the site uses AJAX to dynamically load new items. Although the search functionality has not yet been implemented, it's a feature I plan to add to improve navigation and make it easier to search for specific pokemons.</br></br>The main challenge was getting familiar with Angular's routing and providers, as this was my second project with the technology. The experience was essential to consolidate my understanding of Angular development and enhance my skills in building complex applications.</br></br>This project is aimed at Pokémon fans looking for a simple and efficient way to access detailed information about their favorite pokemons.

title: Blog Pessoal
description: I developed a personal blog to learn about modern web development. Using HTML, CSS, and Angular, I created a blog with reusable components and mocked data, without API integration. The project helped me better understand website and system development.
text: The 'Blog Pessoal' project was developed with the goal of deepening my knowledge in modern web development, stepping out of the traditional PHP, WordPress, and vanilla JavaScript environment. In this project, I used HTML, CSS, and Angular to create a functional blog without the need for API integration, using mock data for simulation.</br></br>The blog features a main page displaying reusable blog cards. Each card is a unique component that adapts based on the received parameters, allowing a consistent display of image, title, and description. The main page includes a list with a 'load more' button, and the internal page displays the main text of each post.</br></br>The biggest challenge was developing a reusable blog component that met the project's needs without creating multiple distinct cards. The solution was creating a versatile component that adapts to the provided information, ensuring efficiency and simplified maintenance.</br></br>Although the project doesn't have a specific target audience, it was fundamental in helping me better understand modern web development practices, expanding my experience with Angular and responsive design techniques.

title: VoxAI
description: VoxAI is a revolutionary WordPress plugin that integrates the power of artificial intelligence directly into your editor, allowing you to generate automated summaries, audio, and translations for your posts.
text: VoxAI was born out of the need to automate repetitive processes in content creation. Developed with a total focus on user experience and performance, this plugin connects your WordPress to the best artificial intelligence APIs on the market.</br></br>With it, we transform posts into accessible audio experiences and make content producers' lives easier with automatic generation of metadata and executive summaries.
